info.description:
The Alvera Platform API provides programmatic access to datalake management, data ingestion, identity verification, and agentic workflow execution.

Endpoints require `X-API-Key` (M2M) or `Authorization: Bearer` (human sessions) for authentication, except Health and session creation.

For detailed setup and configuration, see the platform how-to guides:
- [Tenant Management](/guides/howto_manage_tenants.html)
- [Datalake Management](/guides/howto_manage_datalakes.html)
- [Data Activation](/guides/howto_manage_data_activation.html)
- [Workflows](/guides/howto_manage_workflows.html)
info.title: Alvera Platform API

get.description:
Public health check endpoint that returns:
- Application version
- Database connectivity status (SELECT 1 query)
- Current timestamp

**No authentication required.**
Search a dataset by type with Flop-based pagination and filtering. The dataset parameter is the dataset type atom (e.g. patient, member, appointment).

parameters.description: Optional datalake ID (defaults to first datalake for the org)
parameters.name: datalake_id

delete.description:
Revokes the current Bearer session — deactivates the session and deletes
the linked UserToken. The Bearer token becomes immediately invalid.

Only works with `Authorization: Bearer <session_token>` (not X-API-Key).

**Requires Bearer authentication.**

post.description:
Exchange user credentials for a Bearer session token.

Send `{email, password, tenant_slug}` to authenticate a human user and receive
a session token. Use the returned `session_token` as
`Authorization: Bearer <session_token>` on subsequent API requests.

Optionally specify `expires_in` (seconds) to control session duration.
Default: 86400 (24 hours). Maximum: 2592000 (30 days).

For M2M (server-to-server) authentication, use `X-API-Key` header directly on
each request instead — no session exchange needed.

**No authentication required** — this endpoint authenticates the caller.

requestBody.description: User credentials

get.description:
Returns the current session's tenant, role, and identity information.

Works with both authentication methods:
- `X-API-Key` header (M2M)
- `Authorization: Bearer <session_token>` (human sessions)

Use this as a "who am I" endpoint to confirm credentials are valid and
inspect the resolved tenant, role, and identity.

**Requires authentication** (X-API-Key or Bearer token).

Returns a paginated list of tenants accessible to the authenticated identity.

- **Bearer token**: returns all tenants the user has membership in.
- **X-API-Key**: returns the single tenant the API key is scoped to.
Returns a paginated list of action status updaters for the authenticated tenant.

post.description: Creates a new action status updater for the authenticated tenant.

requestBody.description: Full Action Status Updater resource (PUT semantics — all fields required)

put.description: Replaces an action status updater with the full resource body. PUT semantics — all required fields must be present.

requestBody.description: Full Action Status Updater resource (PUT semantics — all fields required)

post.description: Executes a single workflow action identified by decision_key for the given dataset record, bypassing filter and decision evaluation

requestBody.description: Execute payload

post.summary: Execute an individual action for a dataset record

post.description:
Given a connected app slug and a Puid short URL token, resolves the token to
the pre-stored route path and MDM subject ID. The Cloudflare app calls this
endpoint when a customer clicks a short URL, forwarding the original client
headers (user-agent, IP, country) in the request body.

When the page token is linked to a message (via workflow action execution),
the regulated message details (raw body, channel, status) are included in
the response.

**Requires X-API-Key authentication.**

requestBody.description: Page resolution request

post.summary: Resolve a short URL token to a page

put.description:
Updates tracking timestamps (opened_at, form_submitted_at) on the message
linked to a page token. Called by the connected app when a user opens a page
or submits a form.

**Requires X-API-Key authentication.**

requestBody.description: Page update request

put.summary: Update message tracking fields for a page

post.description: Ingest a single JSON record for processing through the data activation pipeline

requestBody.description: JSON payload

post.description: Ingests a file that was uploaded via a presigned URL obtained from the datalake upload-link endpoint. The key must belong to this client's datalake prefix (`uploads/<datalake_id>/...`).

requestBody.description: Ingest file request

requestBody.description: Full Generic Table resource (PUT semantics — all fields required)

post.description:
Verify a subject's identity against their master record using fuzzy matching.

Supports verification by:
- **family_name** — Jaro distance >= 0.85
- **given_name** — Jaro distance >= 0.80
- **birth_date** — Fuzzy component matching >= 0.80
- **identifiers** — Exact (system, value) match

At least one verification field is required.

**Requires X-API-Key authentication.**

requestBody.description: Verification attributes

post.description: Returns a presigned PUT URL for uploading a file (NDJSON or CSV) directly to the datalake's regulated cloud storage. The returned key lives under `uploads/<datalake_id>/` and can be passed to downstream endpoints (e.g. data activation client ingest-file) that accept a pre-uploaded storage key.

requestBody.description: Upload link request
